title: Delivery worker circuit breaker and retry backoff
description:
src/delivery.rs: the delivery subsystem has a well-designed failure handling strategy worth keeping:

1. Exponential retry backoff (60s, 5m, 30m, 2h, 8h) with max 7 attempts before dead-lettering — prevents thundering herd on a down server
2. Per-domain circuit breaker (10 consecutive failures trips for 1 hour) — prevents a single down domain from monopolizing the delivery worker
3. 410 Gone handling automatically removes followers and cleans up the delivery queue — respects instance shutdown signals
4. Breaker cooldown resets the counter, giving the domain a fresh retry window rather than permanent blacklisting
5. Dead-letter queue with CLI tools (inspect, retry, stats) for operational visibility

The circuit breaker and retry schedule together mean a temporarily-down instance gets retried politely, a permanently-gone instance is cleaned up, and a flapping instance doesn't waste delivery cycles.
